skill.name: testing-qa
skill.description: Coordinate testing and QA work across unit, integration, E2E, browser automation, performance, review, and release quality gates.
skill.summary: Plan and execute a complete software testing and QA workflow.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, screenshot wording, weak-crypto keywords, and a testing pyramid diagram as high-risk patterns. Manual review found no executable code, shell commands, network calls, file writes, credential access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempt in SKILL.md. The remaining risk is low because the workflow asks users to rely on many separately reviewed skills.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections are false positives. The cited lines are Markdown inline code spans, fenced prompt examples, or skill names, not Ruby or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is Markdown guidance and contains no executable Ruby, shell script, or command substitution. The flagged backticks wrap skill names and copy-paste prompts.

low_findings.title: High-Severity Keyword Detections Are Contextual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, screen capture upload, and system reconnaissance alerts are false positives. The cited text describes QA workflow steps, screenshot capture, and a testing pyramid diagram, not cryptographic code, upload behavior, or host reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review shows ordinary testing and documentation language at each cited line. No code path implements weak cryptography, screenshot upload, or system reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Workflow Delegates to Many External Skills
low_findings.description: The workflow asks users to invoke many named skills for testing, automation, screenshots, performance, review, and security scanning. This is not malicious, but each referenced skill must be trusted and reviewed separately.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dependency boundary is clear because the skill explicitly names many other skills. The impact depends on those external skills, so the risk is real but indirect.

content.user_title: Build Reliable Test and QA Workflows
content.value_statement: Software teams need repeatable testing coverage across many layers before release. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured QA workflow from strategy through quality gates.
actual_capabilities: Outlines a phased QA workflow from test strategy to release quality gates.
Identifies which specialized testing skills to use for each workflow phase.
Covers unit, integration, E2E, browser, performance, review, and validation tasks.
Provides copy-paste prompts for common testing and QA activities.
Includes a practical quality gates checklist for release readiness.
limitations: Does not include executable test code or framework configuration files.
Depends on separate skills for implementation details and browser automation.
Does not verify test results or coverage by itself.
Requires project-specific adaptation for languages, frameworks, and CI systems.

use_cases.title: Set Up QA for a New Feature Team
use_cases.description: Create a shared testing plan that covers unit tests, integration tests, E2E scenarios, and release checks.

use_cases.title: Expand Automated Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to choose the next testing layer and coordinate test generation with specialized skills.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Release Quality Review
use_cases.description: Apply the quality gates checklist before shipping changes to production.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Test Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a project-level QA plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-qa workflow to create a test plan for this project. Include unit, integration, E2E, and release quality gates.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Unit Test Coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request focused on unit testing gaps.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-qa workflow to identify missing unit tests and suggest which testing skills should help implement them.

prompt_templates.title: Design Browser and E2E Coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for user journey validation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-qa workflow to design E2E and browser automation coverage for critical user journeys in this application.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Release Quality Gate
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for CI and release readiness.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-qa workflow to define release quality gates, required checks, and review steps for a production deployment.

output_examples.input: Plan testing for a new checkout flow.
output: A phased QA plan covering unit tests for pricing logic, integration tests for payment services, and E2E tests for checkout completion.
A browser automation checklist for responsive behavior and visual regression.
A release gate list with coverage, security scan, linting, and review checks.

output_examples.input: Find gaps in our current testing approach.
output: A gap analysis across unit, integration, E2E, performance, and code review coverage.
Recommended next skills to invoke for each missing test layer.
A prioritized action list for improving quality before release.

best_practices: Start with a clear test strategy before generating individual tests.
Map each critical user path to at least one automated validation layer.
Keep quality gates explicit, measurable, and tied to release decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not treat screenshot capture as proof that functional behavior is correct.
Do not add E2E tests before core unit and integration coverage exists.
Do not invoke referenced skills without checking their own security posture.

faq.question: Does this skill write tests directly?
faq.answer: No. It coordinates testing work and points to specialized skills that can help write or improve tests.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it support Playwright workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes an E2E and browser automation phase that references Playwright-oriented testing work.

faq.question: Can it replace a QA engineer?
faq.answer: No. It provides structure, prompts, and checklists, but humans still review risk, coverage, and release readiness.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found only Markdown instructions and indirect dependency risk from referenced skills.

faq.question: What should teams review before using it?
faq.answer: Teams should review the referenced skills, project test requirements, CI limits, and any browser automation permissions.
